reverse_geocoded.formatted_address: Via Ricasoli, 99 R, 50121 Firenze FI, Italy

details.description: A monumental white marble statue of a nude male stands on a square marble pedestal inside a domed gallery. The figure is rendered with precise anatomical detail in a contrapposto pose, weight on the right leg, left hand raised holding a sling over the shoulder, and a small tree-stump support behind the right leg. The surrounding space is an elegant circular/semicircular hall with tall paneled walls and a coffered dome above, painted in a pale neutral tone. Soft, diffuse overhead lighting illuminates the sculpture. The vantage is slightly low, with the tops of visitors visible at the bottom edge.

entities.name: David (Michelangelo)
entities.notes: Marble sculpture by Michelangelo, 1501–1504, High Renaissance; approx. 5.17 m tall; carved from Carrara marble; depicts the biblical hero David in contrapposto; originally installed at Piazza della Signoria, moved to the Accademia in 1873.

details.description: A large, highly polished white marble sculpture of a nude young man stands on a square pedestal in a bright, circular interior. The statue is in contrapposto, weight on one leg, with the left arm raised toward the chest and the right arm relaxed at the side. Intricate muscular detail and curly hair are rendered with classical realism. The surrounding space is a museum hall with pale paneled walls and a smooth, domed ceiling featuring geometric recessed panels. The lighting is soft and even, emphasizing the statue’s form while the room appears quiet and gallery-like. The scene conveys Renaissance sculpture in a dedicated display room, likely inside a Florence museum.

details.description: Interior museum view of Michelangelo’s marble statue David standing in contrapposto on a square plinth beneath a segmented, coffer-like domed rotunda. The nude figure looks left, his left hand holding the sling over his shoulder and a rough tree-stump support behind his right leg. The light, paneled walls and dome, overhead museum lighting, and the tops of visitors confirm the dedicated Tribuna space of the Galleria dell’Accademia in Florence.

details.description: A full-length marble statue of David by Michelangelo, nude and posed in contrapposto with weight on the right leg, left hand resting near the shoulder and a calm, focused gaze. The sculpture stands on a stone pedestal inside a museum gallery with pale paneled walls, consistent with the Florence location that houses Michelangelo's David (Galleria dell'Accademia).

entities.name: David (Michelangelo)
entities.notes: Sculpted by Michelangelo, circa 1501–1504; marble statue; housed in Galleria dell'Accademia, Florence.

details.description: A vertical, low-angle full shot of Michelangelo's David, the renowned Renaissance marble sculpture. The nude male figure stands in a classic contrapposto pose, resting his weight on his right leg while the left is relaxed. His head is turned to the left with a focused, intense expression, and his left hand holds a sling draped over his shoulder. The statue is situated indoors within a specially designed architectural niche, known as the Tribune, featuring a light-colored, coffered semi-dome ceiling and neoclassical wall paneling. Soft, diffuse natural light illuminates the statue from above, accentuating the anatomical detail and musculature. The tops of visitors' heads are barely visible in the bottom right corner.

details.description: A full-length view of Michelangelo's David, a masterpiece of Renaissance sculpture. The white marble statue depicts a nude male figure in a contrapposto stance, with his weight on his right leg and his head turned to his left. He holds a sling over his left shoulder. The statue is positioned under a large, coffered dome with a skylight, within a curved architectural niche featuring vertical wall panels and decorative moldings. The lighting is soft and diffused, highlighting the anatomical details of the sculpture.

details.description: A monumental white marble nude male statue in contrapposto stands on a tall rectangular marble pedestal inside a museum hall. The figure holds a stone in his right hand and a sling draped over his left shoulder. The space is an apsidal gallery with a coffered semi-dome and paneled vaults in pale gray tones, lit by diffuse indoor lighting. The statue is Michelangelo’s David displayed in the Tribuna of the Galleria dell’Accademia in Florence.

details.description: A life-size white marble sculpture of a nude male standing in a contrapposto pose. The figure is highly idealized with detailed musculature, curly hair, and a calm, focused expression. He stands on a rectangular plinth indoors, with soft, even museum lighting and a plain, paneled interior in the background, suggesting a classical gallery setting. The sculpture resembles Michelangelo's David, a renowned Renaissance masterpiece, known for its technical perfection and balanced proportions.

entities.name: Michelangelo's David
entities.notes: Artist: Michelangelo Buonarroti; Date: c. 1501–1504; Material: marble; Current location: Galleria dell'Accademia, Florence

details.description: The image shows Michelangelo’s marble statue David displayed indoors on a tall square pedestal. The nude figure stands in contrapposto with weight on the right leg, the left leg relaxed, left arm bent with the sling resting on the shoulder, and the right hand lowered holding a stone. A carved tree-trunk support is behind the right leg. The setting is the museum’s apse/rotunda with pale gray paneled walls and a coffered semi-dome, photographed from a low angle with a visitor silhouette at the lower right—consistent with the Tribuna housing the original David in Florence’s Galleria dell’Accademia.

details.description: A low-angle, full-length photograph of Michelangelo's David, a colossal Renaissance marble sculpture. The nude male figure stands in a contrapposto pose, with his weight on his right leg and his left leg relaxed. His head is turned to his left with a focused, intense expression and furrowed brow. His left hand holds a sling draped over his shoulder, while his right hand hangs by his side. The statue is situated indoors within a rotunda (the Tribuna) featuring a high, vaulted ceiling with a skylight and walls decorated with neoclassical molding and recessed panels. The lighting is natural and diffuse, entering from above.

details.description: A low-angle, vertical-oriented photograph of Michelangelo's 'David' statue. The white marble sculpture depicts a nude male figure in a contrapposto stance, with his weight shifted onto his right leg. His left arm is raised toward his shoulder, and his right arm hangs by his side. The statue is set against a backdrop of a high, vaulted ceiling with recessed rectangular panels (coffering). The lighting is bright and even, highlighting the detailed musculature and smooth texture of the marble. The statue stands on a marble pedestal.
